title: Black-hole complementarity & its consistency
statement: Black-hole complementarity (Susskind, Thorlacius, Uglum 1993; 't Hooft) proposes that the descriptions seen by an infalling observer (who passes a smooth horizon) and an external observer (who sees information thermalized and re-emitted on a 'stretched horizon') are complementary, not contradictory, because no single observer can access both. It was the leading reconciliation of unitarity with a smooth horizon until the firewall argument (#06) challenged its consistency. Whether complementarity is internally consistent, and how it is realized, is open.

originators.name: Gerard 't Hooft
originators.note: independent precursor ideas on horizon information and the holographic S-matrix.

ranking.rationale: A specific proposed mechanism (not a brute mystery), now partly superseded/challenged by firewalls and reframed by holography; ranked mid-list as an actively-debated consistency question rather than a deep open void.

key_ideas: No-cloning is respected because no observer sees information both inside and in the radiation.
The 'stretched horizon' is a fictitious membrane (a Planck length outside the true horizon) that absorbs, thermalizes and re-radiates information for the external observer.
Complementarity underlies and motivated the holographic principle.
Hayden-Preskill: a black hole that has already radiated half its entropy returns infalling information very quickly (the black hole as a near-optimal scrambler).
The firewall argument (#06) claims complementarity is inconsistent for old black holes; ER=EPR (#08) is a proposed rescue.
AdS/CFT gives a manifestly unitary external description, sharpening the question of how the interior is encoded.

approaches.name: Stretched-horizon membrane
approaches.summary: Model the horizon as a dissipative membrane for external observers (the membrane paradigm made quantum).

approaches.name: Holographic encoding (AdS/CFT)
approaches.summary: The interior is encoded in boundary CFT data; subregion/entanglement-wedge reconstruction.

approaches.name: Fast scrambling / Hayden-Preskill
approaches.summary: Quantify how quickly information is returned, constraining when complementarity must hold.

papers.title: The Stretched Horizon and Black Hole Complementarity
On the Quantum Structure of a Black Hole
papers.note: Nucl. Phys. B 256, 727 — horizon information and the S-matrix approach.

papers.title: Black holes as mirrors: quantum information in random subsystems (Hayden-Preskill)

physicists.name: Gerard 't Hooft
physicists.contribution: Nobel laureate; horizon information, holographic principle precursor.

physicists.name: John Preskill
physicists.contribution: Hayden-Preskill; quantum information of black holes.

simulator_angle: The classical membrane paradigm (a conductive horizon membrane) is a visualization the simulator could render; the quantum complementarity question is beyond it.

metadata.coverage_note: Seed: 5 key references (4 with arXiv ids; 't Hooft 1985 as note) + 6 physicists. Aspiration up to 25 papers, to be expanded.
